failure_surface: Agent wrote data to the wrong sheet ('Result what i am getting') instead of the target sheet specified in answer_position ('MyResult'). Additionally, new rows were appended at the end rather than maintaining sorted order by Unique ID.
root_cause: The agent assumed the sheet name from instruction text matched the answer_position target without verifying if the specified sheet exists. When encountering a non-existent sheet name ('MyResult'), the agent defaulted to modifying the source comparison sheet instead of creating the target sheet or raising an error.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing data, verify that the target sheet specified in answer_position exists in the workbook. If it does not exist, either create it or explicitly report the mismatch rather than silently falling back to an alternative sheet.
evidence: answer_position field specifies 'MyResult'!A1:O20 but wb.sheetnames only contains ['Main unique ID', 'Result what i am getting', 'Result what i am expecting '] - 'MyResult' does not exist
Agent code uses ws_result = wb['Result what i am getting'] hardcoded based on instruction text rather than reading from answer_position
New IDs (8, 9, 47, 99, 500) were written at rows 14-18 after all existing data instead of being inserted in sorted numeric order
Expected output shows IDs should be sorted (7, 8, 9, 12, 15...) but combined_df maintains original order with appends at end

memory_items.title: Target Sheet Validation Before Writing
memory_items.lesson: Always verify the target sheet specified in the task parameters exists in the workbook before attempting to write data to it.
memory_items.generalization: If the target sheet name from answer_position or task instructions does not match any wb.sheetnames entry, either create the sheet programmatically or explicitly report the discrepancy to the user rather than defaulting to an alternate sheet.

memory_items.title: Maintain Sorted Order When Merging ID Columns
memory_items.lesson: When combining data frames based on a key column like Unique ID, ensure the final output maintains a consistent sorted order unless otherwise specified.
memory_items.generalization: After concatenating dataframes with new rows, sort the combined dataframe by the primary key column (e.g., Unique ID) before writing to preserve logical ordering and meet expected output format requirements.

memory_items.title: Separate Instruction Text from Structural Parameters
memory_items.lesson: Distinguish between descriptive instruction text and formal structural parameters like answer_position which define exact targets.
memory_items.generalization: Prioritize explicit structural parameters (sheet names in answer_position, cell ranges, output paths) over natural language descriptions when determining where to write data, as instructions may contain legacy or inconsistent terminology.

edits.content:
## 6. Target Validation
- **Sheet Existence Check**: Before writing to any target sheet, confirm its existence via `wb.sheetnames`. If the target does not exist, either create it with `wb.create_sheet()` or report the mismatch.
- **Structural Parameter Priority**: Use explicit structural parameters (cell ranges in answer_position, specified output paths) rather than inferring targets from instruction text alone, as they may use different naming conventions.
- **Sorted Output After Merge**: When merging or appending rows based on a key column (like IDs), always sort the combined dataframe by that key before writing to ensure predictable, ordered output matching expected formats.
